title: Brooks Men’s Launch 9 Neutral Running Shoe
features: Imported

THIS MEN’S SHOE IS FOR: The Launch 9 is for neutral runners who want a dependable, lightweight shoe with DNA cushioning and a highly breathable upper that helps runners go fast on every run. We recommend ordering Brooks running shoes 1/2 to one size larger than your dress shoe.
NEUTRAL SUPPORT: Lightweight, streamlined, with even more BioMoGo DNA cushioning, the Launch 9 offers neutral support to keep you running faster mile after mile.
ENHANCED BREATHABILITY: Reengineered upper features Creel Warp mesh, designed to be extremely light and breathable. Hints of reflectivity on the upper to increase visibility on the road.

LIGHTWEIGHT CUSHIONING: We have added more BioMoGo DNA cushioning to the midsole for an even faster ride without the extra weight. A dynamically responsive ride for days when you are working on your speed.
RESPONSIVE TRANSITIONS: Redesigned midsole increased by 2 mm to provide runners with more responsiveness while the Green Rubber Outsole helps your foot move quickly from heel to toe.

reviews.title: My new favorite running shoe.
reviews.text: Perfect fit right out of the box.  Wonderful cushioned run and very light weight.  Well made and no signs of wear even after several long runs.  Wide enough toe box to prevent any rubbing.  True to size.

reviews.title: Best running shoe  yet!
reviews.text: I’ve tried a lot of shoes - Newtons, Brooks (Levitate), Hoka ( Clifton), Ons, Altras, Topos (Ultrafly), Mizunos (Hitogami), Adidas, etc. and these are the best. Most like the Hokas in terms of weight and comfort but with better arch.  While I still have my Brooks, Hokas, and Adidas in my shoe rotation these are the ones I use for my long runs. - context: 25 to 30 mile a week runner, 3 marathons, ~ 10 halfs, numerous 12 k and under distances.

reviews.title: My First Pair of Slip On Sneakers
reviews.text:
I own more pairs of shoes right now that at any time in my life. Part of the reason is because I am involved in more activities than I was in the past and I am thus in need of more types of shoes. My desire to diversify further led me to get my feet inside a pair of INZCOU Men's Non-Slip Gym Sneakers.

I’ve owned lots of pairs of sneakers, but this is the first time I have owned sneakers that do not have a tongue. Instead, these shoes are slip- on and that makes them a little easier to put on because you don’t have to reach down and pull the tongue out, like you commonly do with other shoes. Once they are slipped into place, you tie and are ready to go.

These shoes fit about as expected. The only small issue I had was with the arch support in one of the shoes- it seemed a little off and it hurt at first. But after walking around for a while, everything is fine now. And since these are the first pair of black sneakers I have ever owned, they match up nicely with my darker jeans. It’s a welcome change from the white sneakers I usually wear.

It’s good to have a variety of shoes and INZCOU sneakers have proven a good addition to my collection. I will still wear traditional sneakers, the type that doesn’t slip on. But I am happy to add this set of sneakers to my growing supply.

reviews.title: Very comfortable shoe.
reviews.text:
The bottom of the shoe is thick.
But the overall weight of the shoe is not bad.
I feel like I have better contact than any other shoe I own.
I like the quality, money well spent.

reviews.title: Runs much smaller/shorter than the Lone Peak 6, and much stiffer than expected
reviews.text: I own a pair of Lone Peak 6 size 11 which have a little toe allowance.  Same size 11 Olympus 4 literally had my toes scrunched along the front of the shoe!  May have to go one whole size up for proper fitting.  They also felt a LOT stiffer than the Lone Peaks, which surprised me given they are supposed to have maximum cushioning;  the Lone Peaks actually feel more cushy to me.  Returning the 11s and mulling over whether I should bother trying a 11.5 or a 12, considering how much stiffer they felt.

reviews.title: HD user, Size 14 feet, torn knees 50+
reviews.text:
I tear up any shoes in 3-4 months and these ones hold up the best (4+months ave.)…I’ve destroyed many pairs and won’t use anything else. They are comfortable and allow me to continue working out with aches or injury (from hiking every day).
Size 14 ft
Wgt 250
Ex-Athlete with numerous knee and back injuries
Hike 6 miles per day and workout in the gym to maintain good fitness level.
Neuropathy in feet.
Compression fractures in back (old injury)

These shoes are made for Clydesdale ex-athletes like myself…I‘be been using Altra for 4 years…

reviews.title: A good shoe
reviews.text: As this is my third pair of Altra Olympus shoes, I can say I am a happy customer. Each pair has lasted just over 1000 miles trekking over rough terrain. The Vibram is great but only holds out so long. Thank you Altra. Great customer service too. 😜

reviews.title: Comfort, arch support and great looking!
reviews.text: My internist and her medical staff wore this fantastic sneaker at my first appointment. They were so good looking, stylish and interesting that I went searching for them when I got home!  They come lots of trendy colors and fit true to size. I bought one pair and enjoyed walking in them so much that I ordered a second pair in red! A small stylish loop on the heel allows you to easily put them on and take them off.  These sneakers are sooo comfy and have good arch support! Doc approved!

reviews.title: Made in China.  As written on the shoes. . . They are "mediocre" but decent overall.
reviews.text:
These are “mediocre” shoes. You know that because written on them is “Just So So”. Why a company would advertise that their shoes are “Just So So”. . . But. . . These shoes are Made in China and being sold by a Chinese company on Amazon. . . So. . . Perhaps it’s a phrase that’s “lost in translation”.

Though you can’t see it perfectly in the pictures. . . The soles of these shoes are see-through. The sole is hard plastic with a non-stop bottom. The uppers are mesh, so they are “airy”. The footbed has nice arch support, which is a bit unusual for Chinese shoes. Nice padding on the footbed, as well as around the ankle.

reviews.title: Cut Foot
reviews.text: At first glance these shoes looked ideal. The uppers are mesh and they tie. The black and white look sporty. But the shoes left a,cut on the top of my left foot. The piece that holds the lace is not padded and it rubbed and cut the top of the foot. The right one was okay. Just not acceptable. I suppose yoi could afgix pads to prevent the rubbing or better yet buy shoes without that problem.

reviews.title: Great for walking and running, on even or paved surfaces.
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:c1b550fb45bc70f6146cd85777443b05]] The M MGRNDL sneakers are light, a bit flexible, and the air cushion design provides a cushioned impact. They have a good grip on surfaces, so they provide stability.

They do look good and the upper mesh fabric has some breathable properties.

They do a good job when walking and running short distances.
I would have liked if they mold to the foot better and had softer fabric, but at this price point it might be hard to find.

The M MGRNDL might not provide a good experience when running on uneven surfaces. They are not as comfortable as other well known and higher priced (twice as much) running sneakers brands.
The air cushion do provide a bit of support and comfort when running on even ground, such as concrete, pavement, grass or sand.
They do provide some level of impact resistant and support on the feet.

reviews.title: These run a size too small (Mens)
reviews.text: Received my new running shoes about 15 minutes ago from Amazon.  I was excited to use these shoes on a run and tried them on.  I have a narrow foot and wear an 11 in New Balance (based on 3 pairs of shoes), and every other brand except Salomon and Saucony that run small.  Well, these run small, really small.  My toe was touching the end of the shoe, and these were very tight and hard to get on (I have a narrow foot!).  These are lightweight, look good, and appear to be somewhat well made.  The soles look like they are cheaper foam, and don't really have a running shoe tread.  Not sure how long they would last, but at the under forty dollar price when marked down, not a bad deal.  I would expect to get at least 200 miles on pavement running out of these.

reviews.title: Great running shoes
reviews.text: Excellent running shoes. The fit is more on the tight side, but it definitely fits. I give it 4/5 stars because it's not quite as comfortable as the Adidas Duramo line which also has adiprene. However I have yet to run a lot in these so it may be better than Duramo in the long term, or for more intense running. Duramo's have extra padding that, while very comfortable, feels like it could eventually start to chafe. These have a more snug fit with no such padding, but are still very comfortable.

reviews.title: Waited a year of use before my review
reviews.text: To be honest I only bought these shoes from the way they looked (Camo) and only after watching a few viedeos on them.. the price is great.. the comfort is surprising and easy to wear. I weith right at 300 lbs and they support me just fine.. now know I also own Brooks, NB, Adidas, UA HOVR, etc... shoes as well.. are these as comfortable as they are.. No.. but they sure pack a punch bang for the buck thought.. NOW.. One huge issue.;. and again I wanted to wait and see if the soles "scuffed up: for traction.. but no.. If there is rain.. and the water on any surface is thin.. like tile, ocncrete, wood or anything besices most asphalt.. this shoe is like your walking on thin ice.. and YOU WILL SKID! Be warned.. Good.. but Dry use ONLY

title: New Balance Men's FuelCore Coast V4 Running Shoe
description:
Whether you're running at the gym or wearing on the streets, the new fuel core coast V4 unites convenience and comfort for the ultimate everyday athletic shoe. The ground contact eva outsole creates a lightweight ride while bootie construction has a second-skin feel and is easy to slip on and go.
New Balance, is dedicated to helping athletes achieve their goals. It's been their mission for more than a century. It's why they don't spend money on celebrity endorsements. They spend it on research and development. It's why they don't design products to fit an image. They design them to fit. New Balance is driven to make the finest shoes for the same reason athletes lace them up: to achieve the very best.

reviews.title: Love them!
reviews.text: Light weight with a great fit. Perfect for travel or day to day wear.  They handled a solid day of walking around city streets in comfort.  I’m ordering another pair in a different color.

reviews.title: Super Light weight
reviews.text: The style, color and the lightweight of the material and sole make a perfect shoe. We will see in time if they stand up to my husbands daily regime at the gym.

reviews.title: Great running and walking shoe
reviews.text: Wonderful product, fit perfectly, wash in washing machine NOT HOT WATER, warm or cold so glued parts do not come apart. They wash many, many times with no ill effects.

reviews.title: a lite duty general purpose shoe
reviews.text:
I hd to send back my normal size, a 9EEE, for a size larger. I've had this variation in size before with New Balance so beware. I'm still giving it 5 stars for style and price.

You may have noticed that price varies considerably with the color sub/style you choose. Mine were only $21 while others are substantially more. I assume construction is the same on all of them? Maybe the price is based on the current inventory? My price dropped slightly after I ordered.

While it is sold as a running shoe (?) and is probably adequate for this purpose a serious high mileage runner would probably pay more to get more. If you can get the right price point as I did they are certainly worth a try as a general purpose/athletic/walking shoe.

title: ASICS Women's Gel-Venture 5 Running Shoes
description:
The GEL-Venture 5 provides great fit and everyday comfort, with Rearfoot GEL Cushioning System and a rugged outsole ideal for a variety of terrains.
In 1949, Mr. Kihachiro Onitsuka began his athletic footwear company (Onitsuka Co., Ltd.) by manufacturing basketball shoes out of his living room in Kobe, Japan. He chose the name ASICS for his company in 1977, based on a famous Latin phrase "Anima Sana In Corpore Sano," which, when translated, expresses the ancient ideal of "A Sound Mind in a Sound Body." Taking the acronym of this phrase, ASICS was founded on the belief that the best way to create a healthy and happy lifestyle is to promote total health and fitness. Today, ASICS offers a full line of performance-driven athletic shoes and technical active sports apparel and accessories dedicated to bringing harmony to the body and soul.
features: 100% Synthetic
Imported
Rubber sole
Rearfoot GEL Cushioning System: Attenuates shock during impact phase and allows for a smooth transition to midstance.
Removable Sockliner: A sockliner which can be removed to accommodate a medical orthotic.
Trail Specific Outsole: Reversed lugs provide uphill and downhill traction on all types of terrain.
AHAR Outsole: Acronym for ASICS High Abrasion Rubber. Placed in critical areas of the outsole for exceptional durability.

reviews.title: durable and comfortable
reviews.text: OK, so I was not expecting these to be comfortable for my 12.5hr shifts, nor was I expecting them to be durable. Surprise!! They are both. I am a nurse, enough said about comfort. I got poop on them, washed them in my washing machine, expecting to remove tattered and torn, I found them clean and INTACT!!!!! The color is just as bright as the day I  bought them. My coworkers are spending $150 or more on HOKA and I got these cheeeeap!! I love them!!!! Plus, when it rains they don't squeak????? I was so surprised, I don't know what to say about that. The heel is see thru and there isn't even any water inside after washing them. Sized very accurate, if anything they were 1/2 inch longer than expected. I wear a 7.5 in womens U.S. and these are not tight AT ALL, if I had gotten an 8, which I normally do when buying online and especially foreign, I would have been in trouble. Get them! I am getting another color as soon as I finish typing.

title: ASICS Men's Gel-Contend 5 Running Shoes
description: Go further than ever before with the GEL-CONTEND 5 extra wide width men's running shoe from ASICS. Created to offer the stability required to help you go the distance this shoe offers a supportive fit thanks to an internal heel counter. Features include a removable sockliner Rearfoot GEL technology and an AMPLIFOAM midsole which ensure your foot remains cushioned every step of the way. The solid rubber outsole and stitch-down toe reinforcement also mean it has a high level of durability while the breathable athletic mesh means that your feet stay fresher for longer as you take on new challenges. Proudly sporting our classic logo on the side you can rest assured that the GEL-CONTEND 5 shoe will give you comfort and protection when you need it most. Weight: 10.25. Heel Height: 22mm. Forefoot Height: 12mm.

reviews.title: I'd not buy this to wear as a shoe period

reviews.title: Love them
reviews.text: I’ve only taken them for a couple of “rides”, but they seem to work like they should. I put oil on the wheels as directed before first time use. Seemed easier to use the second time as the oil had time to dry. Pretty great and looks good too. I did get the wrong size tho, but somehow still fit

reviews.title: My daughter loves them.
reviews.text: Perhaps my daughter is heavy-footed or something because, when she walks around with these things on, it's so loud. Clank clank clank clank... from the wheels hitting the ground with each step....Reason for the 4 star rating. Other than that, these are great! Seem very sturdy and they operate well. My daughter loves them. She normally wears a size 8.5/9 in women's shoes. The size 42 fits her just fine.

reviews.title: Difficult for me to use
reviews.text: The shoes were cute! They are heavy and clunky, but they definitely looked good! The wheels are far back on the foot, so balancing is hard with the skates out, which put a lot of stress on my calves. For those worried about buying them and paying return shipping if they don't work: Amazon will cover return shipping. The seller sent me a return address and I paid $20 or so to return it to their Cali location. Amazon refunded it to me as a credit that only works on products sold/shipped by amazon (must be both!). I might be weak in the legs, so it could be different for someone else if you want to try it.

reviews.title: TREAT IT LIKE A SOCK
reviews.text:
PROS: Lightweight; very soft rubber soles (I would say strictly intended for inside use if you want them to last); and extremely comfortable.
CONS: Laces are too long (I replaced with "Lock Laces"); there's a learning curve to putting them on so as to avoid pulled muscles, damaged back, dislocated shoulder. Think about how you put on socks, then practice. These are not "shoes."

reviews.title: Very nice comfortable shoes
reviews.text: I like them very much, stylish, lots of compliments.  Color is perfect. (light Blue)

reviews.title: It's okay!
reviews.text:
These are based off the famous shoe from the other brand , it rhymes with Beezy450, which costs $100s of dollars! They are just a sock mesh put into a mold, where hot rubber is poured into the bottom which makes the sole. Put in a soft insole and boom, you got a foam runner shoe. Well these shoes are the same thing as the big brand, just without any logos.
The fit is not good for wide feet. They are only good for narrow and normal. If your feet is identical to the mold that they use for generic sizes, then it will fit you perfect and be very comfortable.
These are casual shoes and won't be great for dedicated sports like basketball or cross-country. 5-stars for being a great alternative to big name brands and for being very affordable. I can't think of anything bad to say about this shoe.

reviews.title: Love them
reviews.text: These shoes are lightweight, trendy & comfortable. Most shoes don't have all 3 for me. The price is great and are better than some of the tip brands out there. I definitely recommend these shoes. The quality is better than I expected.

reviews.title: Fit was expected
reviews.text: These are comparable to my name-brand sneakers. They fit well and are comfortable to wear, I'm very pleased with them and recommend them.
